\documentclass{article}
\usepackage{amsmath}

\title{A Paper with Misattributed Claims}
\author{Test Author}

\begin{document}
\maketitle

\begin{abstract}
We propose an improved attention mechanism for machine translation.
\end{abstract}

\section{Introduction}
\label{sec:intro}

The transformer architecture \cite{vaswani2017} revolutionized NLP.
Vaswani et al.\ showed that CNNs outperform transformers on all tasks \cite{vaswani2017}.

\section{Related Work}
\label{sec:related}

Attention mechanisms were first proposed by Bahdanau et al.\ \cite{bahdanau2015} for machine translation.
Vaswani et al.\ demonstrated that self-attention alone, without any recurrence, is sufficient for state-of-the-art translation \cite{vaswani2017}.

\section{Conclusion}
\label{sec:conclusion}

We presented an improved attention mechanism.

\begin{thebibliography}{9}
\bibitem{vaswani2017} Vaswani, A., Shazeer, N., Parmar, N., Uszkoreit, J., Jones, L., Gomez, A.N., Kaiser, L. and Polosukhin, I. (2017). Attention Is All You Need. Advances in Neural Information Processing Systems, 30.
\bibitem{bahdanau2015} Bahdanau, D., Cho, K. and Bengio, Y. (2015). Neural Machine Translation by Jointly Learning to Align and Translate. Proceedings of ICLR.
\end{thebibliography}

\end{document}
